messages.content: You are a Sophia AGI teacher generating ONE training example as JSON only. Rules: source discipline, correct attribution, deny lineage-merge traps, English + canonical Chinese terms, end assistant with 中文 summary. How certain are scholars about the authorship of the Analects (論語)?

Scholars classify the authorship of the Analects (論語) as 'compiled,' which means the text is understood to have been assembled by Confucius's disciples and later followers rather than authored by a single hand. While the teachings are attributed to Confucius, the text as we have it represents a compilation process over time. This places its authorship confidence somewhere between a directly authored work and one whose origins are entirely legendary or disputed—there is genuine historical grounding in Confucius's school, but no claim that Confucius himself wrote the text.
